status_notes: Initial research from Azure Cosmos DB documentation, 2026-04-04. Needs cross-reference and live environment validation.
notes: Globally distributed multi-model NoSQL database. Security includes encryption, firewall, RBAC, private endpoints.

match_nodes.description: Encryption at rest for all Cosmos DB data

data.description: All data in Cosmos DB is encrypted at rest. Always on and cannot be disabled. Service-managed keys by default; customer-managed keys available via Azure Key Vault for additional control.

options.name: Service-Managed Keys
options.description: Default. Microsoft manages the encryption keys. No additional configuration.

options.name: Customer-Managed Keys
options.description: Customer manages keys in Azure Key Vault. Provides key rotation control and ability to revoke access.

default.note: All data encrypted at rest with service-managed keys. Always on, cannot be disabled.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use customer-managed keys for regulatory compliance requirements.

match_nodes.description: IP-based access control for Cosmos DB accounts

data.description: IP-based access control. When no firewall rules are configured, the account accepts connections from all IP addresses. Adding any rule switches to deny-by-default for unlisted IPs.

default.note: When no IP rules are configured, connections from all IPs are accepted.

data.vendor_recommendation: Configure firewall rules to restrict access. Prefer private endpoints over IP firewall rules for production workloads.

match_nodes.description: Entra ID RBAC for data plane operations

data.description: Microsoft Entra ID role-based access control for data operations. Alternative to primary/secondary key authentication. Supports built-in roles (Cosmos DB Data Reader, Data Contributor) and custom role definitions.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use RBAC with Entra ID instead of primary/secondary keys. Combine with disabling key-based authentication.

audit.cli: az cosmosdb sql role assignment list --account-name {acct} --resource-group {rg}
audit.console: Cosmos DB -> {account} -> Settings -> Keys -> Role-based access control

configure.cli: az cosmosdb sql role assignment create --account-name {acct} --resource-group {rg} --role-definition-id {role-id} --principal-id {principal-id} --scope /

data.description: Access Cosmos DB via Azure Private Link. Assigns a private IP from your VNet. Traffic stays on the Microsoft backbone network. Can disable public network access entirely after configuring.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use private endpoints for all production Cosmos DB accounts. Disable public network access after configuring private endpoints.

audit.console: Cosmos DB -> {account} -> Settings -> Networking -> Private access

match_nodes.description: Disable primary/secondary key authentication to force Entra ID

data.description: Disable primary and secondary key-based authentication. Forces all clients to use Entra ID RBAC. Prevents use of connection strings with account keys.

default.note: Key-based authentication is enabled by default.

data.vendor_recommendation: Disable key-based authentication after configuring Entra ID RBAC for all clients.

data.description: Continuous backup mode with point-in-time restore. Two tiers: 7-day retention (free for most) and 30-day retention. Restores create a new account.

default.note: Default is periodic backup mode (not continuous). Must be changed at account creation or migrated.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use continuous backup mode for production workloads to enable point-in-time restore.

match_nodes.description: Restrict access to specific VNet subnets

data.description: Restrict access to Cosmos DB from specific Azure VNet subnets via service endpoints. Only traffic from the specified subnets is allowed.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use VNet service endpoints or private endpoints to restrict network access. Private endpoints are preferred over service endpoints.

audit.console: Cosmos DB -> {account} -> Settings -> Networking -> Virtual networks
